name: Butternut Squash and Ancho Chili Puree
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the ancho chili in a small saucepan and cover with water. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer until the chili is soft, about 10 minutes. Remove from liquid. When cool enough to handle, stem the chili, split it open, and scrape out the seeds.
2. Put the cubed squash in a saucepan and cover with water. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Reduce heat and simmer until the squash is tender, about 10 minutes. Drain well and transfer to a food processor.
3. Add the chili, cumin, salt, and cayenne to the food processor. Process until smooth, stopping occasionally to scrape down the sides of the bowl.
4. Divide the puree among 4 plates and serve immediately.
